llm_keywords.evidence: |I| changes from 8.90×10^-103 A at -0.1 V to 2.40×10^-5 A at -6 V, a span of about 97.43 decades.
As |V| increases from 0.1 V to 6 V, |I| increases overall from about 10^-103 A to about 10^-5 A on both bias polarities.
Matched |V| values show similar |I| magnitudes, for example 6 V: 2.399×10^-5 A vs 2.249×10^-5 A, 1 V: 6.62×10^-15 A vs 7.83×10^-15 A, and 0.2 V: 1.74×10^-54 A vs 1.73×10^-54 A.
At larger |V|, adjacent points are not strictly monotonic; for example on the positive side |I| goes 1.856×10^-5 A at 5.45 V, 1.795×10^-5 A at 5.50 V, and 1.300×10^-5 A at 5.55 V.
ln(|I|/V^2) changes overall from about -230.38 at -0.1 V and -230.63 at +0.1 V to about -14.22 at -6 V and -14.29 at +6 V.
